prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The World Cultural Council held its 31st Award Ceremony at the Dipoli Hall, Otakaari 1 Building, Aalto University, Espoo, Finland on November 17, 2014. Aalto University was the host and co-organizer of the event, which marked the start of the Aalto University Academic Summit titled "The Impact of Universities on Economic Growth."
B. The World Cultural Council performed its 31st Award Ceremony on November 17, 2014 at the Dipoli Hall, Otakaari 1 Building, Aalto University, Espoo, Finland. Aalto University acted as the host and co-organizer of the event, which was the opening act prior to the Aalto University Academic Summit titled "The Impact of Universities on Economic Growth."The WCC presented awards to two distinguished scientists. Acclaimed enzymologist Professor Sir Philip Cohen received the Albert Einstein World Award of Science for his forty-year research into cellular communication. Professor Federico Rosei accepted the José Vasconcelos World Award of Education, granted for his excellence in the field of university teaching. Jussi Nuorteva, Director of the National Archives of Finland and member of the WCC, presented the Winners' Book to the laureates. Professor Sir Philip
C. On November 17, 2014, the World Cultural Council conducted its 31st Award Ceremony. This event took place at the Dipoli Hall, located in the Otakaari 1 Building, within the Aalto University campus in Espoo, Finland. Aalto University played a dual role, serving as both the host and a co-organizer for the ceremony. This gathering served as the inaugural activity leading up to the Aalto University Academic Summit, an event specifically focused on "The Impact of Universities on Economic Growth." During the ceremony, the WCC recognized two prominent scientists with awards. The highly respected enzymologist, Professor Sir Philip Cohen, was honored with the Albert Einstein World Award of Science. This award acknowledged his four decades of dedicated research into the intricacies of cellular communication. Furthermore, Professor Federico Rosei was presented with the José Vasconcelos World Award of Education, an accolade bestowed upon him for his exceptional contributions to university teaching. Jussi Nuorteva, who holds the position of Director of the National Archives of Finland and is also a member of the WCC, had the distinction of presenting the Winners' Book to the esteemed laureates, including Professor Sir Philip.

Answer:
